property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: FSM transitions only transmit derived SPAKE2+ values (pA, pB, cA, cB); passcode remains in local state variables only
T01 explicitly calls Crypto_DRBG(len=256) to generate InitiatorRandom before any message transmission
T05 explicitly calls Crypto_DRBG(len=256) to generate ResponderRandom after validating passcodeID
T01 and T05 call generate_unique_session_id() which per FSM definition SHALL NOT overlap with existing sessions
T04 transition with guard 'received_passcode_id != 0' leads to R_Error_InvalidParameter with StatusReport(FAILURE)
T11 and T12 are exclusive transitions: T11 (computed_cB != received_cB) → error, T12 (computed_cB == received_cB) → success
T14 and T15 are exclusive transitions: T14 (received_cA != expected_cA) → error, T15 (received_cA == expected_cA) → success
I_AwaitingPakeFinished state has invariant 'encrypted_data_blocked == true'; T16 enables encrypted_data_allowed only after PakeFinished
T12 and T15 call derive_session_keys(Ke) which per FSM uses HKDF with salt=[], info=SEKeys_Info
FSM defines I2RKey derivation but post-session encryption is outside FSM scope; spec requirement clear but enforcement is implementation-dependent
FSM defines R2IKey derivation but post-session encryption is outside FSM scope; spec requirement clear but enforcement is implementation-dependent
FSM derives AttestationChallenge but its usage context (device attestation only) is outside PASE protocol FSM scope
T15 and T16 explicitly call initialize_message_counter() as part of session establishment
T15 and T16 set MaxMessageCounter := 0 aligning with spec requirement for synchronized max_message_counter
T02 guard 'commissioning_window_open == true' enforces PASE only during commissioning context
FSM function construct_pbkdf_parameter_set defines range but no explicit guard validates received parameters on initiator side
FSM function defines 16-32 byte salt but no explicit guard validates received salt length on initiator side
T09, T11, T12 compute TT := Crypto_Transcript(PBKDFParamRequest, PBKDFParamResponse, pA, pB) binding all messages
FSM Crypto_P2 function specifies cA = HMAC(K_confirmP, pB), cB = HMAC(K_confirmV, pA) per SPAKE2+
FSM models single session; exchange ID binding is at message layer below FSM abstraction
Status report encryption is outside PASE FSM scope; occurs after session establishment
T05 action compute_pake_values_responder uses pre-existing passcode state variable (explicit assumption)
FSM function compute_pake_values_initiator specifies PBKDF derivation and mod p reduction per spec
FSM function compute_pake_values_responder specifies L = w1 * P with w1 not stored per augmented PAKE
FSM Crypto_Transcript function specifies 'CHIP PAKE V1 Commissioning' context prefix per spec
Replay protection via message counter window is post-session establishment; outside PASE FSM scope
Ke derived from SPAKE2+ shared secret computation; never transmitted (only cA, cB, pA, pB sent)
I2RKey derived locally from Ke via HKDF; never transmitted in any FSM transition
R2IKey derived locally from Ke via HKDF; never transmitted in any FSM transition
Both cB (T11/T12) and cA (T14/T15) verification required before session established; mutual auth via SPAKE2+
Fresh InitiatorRandom (T01) and ResponderRandom (T05) per session; Ke derived from fresh SPAKE2+ values
FSM state machine enforces strict ordering: Idle→I_AwaitingPBKDFParamResponse→...→I_SessionEstablished
T04, T11, T14 all send StatusReport(FAILURE, SECURE_CHANNEL, INVALID_PARAMETER) and transition to error states
FSM derive_session_keys specifies len=3*CRYPTO_SYMMETRIC_KEY_LENGTH_BITS (128 bits each)
FSM derive_session_keys function explicitly specifies salt=[] per spec requirement
TLV parsing is below FSM abstraction level; implementation-dependent behavior
FSM Crypto_pA return type is CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE_BYTES (65 bytes for uncompressed P-256)
FSM Crypto_pB return type is CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE_BYTES (65 bytes for uncompressed P-256)
FSM Crypto_P2 output specifies cA and cB as CRYPTO_HASH_LEN_BYTES (32 bytes for SHA-256)
Session eviction policy is outside PASE protocol FSM scope; occurs at session management layer

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: PBKDFParamRequest only contains InitiatorRandom, InitiatorSessionId, PasscodeId (0), HasPBKDFParameters - no passcode

critical_transitions.actions_analyzed: compute_pake_values_initiator creates w0/w1 locally; send_pake1(pA) transmits only pA
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: pA = x*P + w0*M is a blinded point; passcode and w1 are never sent

critical_transitions.actions_analyzed: send_pake3(computed_cA) transmits only confirmation MAC cA
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: cA = HMAC(KcA, pB) is a MAC value, not passcode or derived secrets

specification_evidence.quote: It is assumed that the initiator has somehow obtained the passcode and that the responder has the relevant Crypto_PAKEValues_Responder corresponding to the passcode before starting a PASE session establishment protocol.
specification_evidence.context: Establishes that passcode is obtained out-of-band, not transmitted during protocol
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Confirms passcode exists before protocol; never transmitted

specification_evidence.quote: Matter uses SPAKE2+ as described in SPAKE2+ as PAKE
specification_evidence.context: SPAKE2+ is a password-authenticated key exchange that never transmits the password
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Protocol design inherently protects passcode from transmission

supporting_assumptions.assumption: SPAKE2+ protocol is secure and never leaks password
supporting_assumptions.where_enforced: All FSM transitions use SPAKE2+ primitives correctly

supporting_assumptions.assumption: Blinding points M and N are nothing-up-my-sleeve values
supporting_assumptions.where_enforced: FSM Crypto_pA and Crypto_pB use these constants

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Explicit guard checks passcodeID != 0 leads to error state with correct status report

critical_transitions.actions_analyzed: Proceeds to generate ResponderRandom and continue protocol
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Only when passcodeID == 0 does protocol continue; guards are mutually exclusive

specification_evidence.quote: Verify passcodeID is set to 0. If verification fails, the responder SHALL send a status report: StatusReport(GeneralCode: FAILURE, ProtocolId: SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode: INVALID_PARAMETER) and perform no further processing.
specification_evidence.context: First step in responder processing of PBKDFParamRequest
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: FSM T04 exactly implements this requirement

supporting_assumptions.assumption: PasscodeID 0 corresponds to current commissioning window verifier

critical_transitions.actions_analyzed: Computes TT and Crypto_P2, verifies mismatch, sends FAILURE status report
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Explicit guard ensures cB mismatch leads to error state

critical_transitions.actions_analyzed: Only proceeds when cB matches; derives session keys and sends Pake3
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Protocol continues only on successful verification; guards are mutually exclusive

specification_evidence.quote: Verify Pake2.cB against cB. If verification fails, the initiator SHALL send a status report: StatusReport(GeneralCode: FAILURE, ProtocolId: SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode: INVALID_PARAMETER) and perform no further processing.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: FSM T11 exactly implements failure case; T12 implements success case

supporting_assumptions.assumption: Crypto_P2 correctly computes cB from transcript per SPAKE2+

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Explicit guard ensures cA mismatch leads to error state

critical_transitions.actions_analyzed: Derives session keys, initializes counters, sends SUCCESS status report
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Session only established on successful cA verification

specification_evidence.quote: Verify Pake3.cA against cA. If verification fails, the responder SHALL send a status report: StatusReport(GeneralCode: FAILURE, ProtocolId: SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode: INVALID_PARAMETER)
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: FSM T14 exactly implements failure case; T15 implements success case

supporting_assumptions.assumption: expected_cA computed during T09 is stored correctly for later comparison

critical_transitions.actions_analyzed: Derives keys but state invariant is 'encrypted_data_blocked == true'
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: I_AwaitingPakeFinished state explicitly blocks encrypted data per invariant

critical_transitions.actions_analyzed: Only after receiving PakeFinished is I_SessionEstablished entered with 'encrypted_data_allowed == true'
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: State transition explicitly enables encrypted data only after PakeFinished

specification_evidence.quote: The initiator SHALL NOT send any encrypted application data until it receives PakeFinished from the responder.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: FSM I_AwaitingPakeFinished state enforces this via invariant

supporting_assumptions.assumption: State invariants are enforced by implementation

critical_transitions.why_partially_holds: Responder generates valid parameters but no guard validates range explicitly
Initiator accepts PBKDF parameters without validating iteration range

fsm_analysis.potential_weakness: Malicious responder could propose iterations below CRYPTO_PBKDF_ITERATIONS_MIN

specification_evidence.quote: iterations: An integer value specifying the number of PBKDF2 iterations: CRYPTO_PBKDF_ITERATIONS_MIN <= iterations <= CRYPTO_PBKDF_ITERATIONS_MAX.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Spec defines valid range but FSM doesn't explicitly validate on receive

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Iterations must be in valid range
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: No explicit validation step on initiator side for received PBKDF parameters
specification_gap.consequence: Initiator may accept malicious low iteration count from rogue responder
specification_gap.security_impact: Weakened offline brute-force resistance if iterations too low

supporting_assumptions.where_stated: Commissioning model assumes device to be commissioned is legitimate

gaps_identified.description: No explicit guard in T06 to validate iterations >= CRYPTO_PBKDF_ITERATIONS_MIN

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Responder authenticated via cB = HMAC(KcB, pA) which requires knowledge of shared secret
Initiator authenticated via cA = HMAC(KcA, pB) which requires knowledge of shared secret

fsm_analysis.authentication_flow: cB verification (responder→initiator) + cA verification (initiator→responder) = mutual auth

specification_evidence.quote: Crypto_P2() SHALL compute cA, cB and Ke as specified in SPAKE2+ with cA := CRYPTO_HMAC(KcA,pB) and cB := CRYPTO_HMAC(KcB,pA).
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Confirmation values cryptographically bind to shared secret derived from passcode

specification_evidence.quote: The Commissioner is the Initiator and the Commissionee is the Responder.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Both roles must verify corresponding confirmation values

supporting_assumptions.assumption: HMAC is unforgeable without knowing the key
KcA and KcB are derived from shared secret only accessible to passcode holders
supporting_assumptions.where_enforced: FSM Crypto_P2 derives keys from transcript

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Each state only has specific valid transitions; cannot skip states
Each state only has specific valid transitions; cannot skip states

specification_evidence.quote: sequenceDiagram: Initiator→Responder: PBKDF Parameter Request, Responder→Initiator: PBKDF Parameter Response, Initiator→Responder: PAKE Contribution, Responder→Initiator: PAKE Contribution/Verification, Initiator→Responder: PAKE Verification, Responder→Initiator: PAKE Finished
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: FSM state transitions exactly match specified sequence

supporting_assumptions.assumption: State machine semantics prevent out-of-order transitions
supporting_assumptions.where_enforced: Each state only accepts specific triggers

identified_gaps.title: Missing PBKDF Parameter Validation on Initiator
identified_gaps.description: The FSM does not include explicit guards for validating PBKDF parameters (iterations and salt length) received by the initiator in PBKDFParamResponse. While the responder generates valid parameters, a malicious responder could propose weak parameters.

specification_gap.what_spec_says: PBKDF iterations SHALL be in valid range; salt SHALL be 16-32 bytes
specification_gap.what_spec_misses: No explicit validation step mandated on initiator side for received parameters

attack_steps: Attacker sets up rogue device to be commissioned
Attacker responds with iterations = 1 (minimum not enforced)
Attacker performs rapid offline brute-force with 1 iteration
attack_scenario.impact: Passcode recovery via offline attack in feasible time

recommendation.spec_change: Add explicit requirement: 'Initiator SHALL verify received PBKDF parameters are within valid ranges before using them'
recommendation.fsm_change: Add guard to T06: received_pbkdf_parameters.iterations >= CRYPTO_PBKDF_ITERATIONS_MIN

mitigating_factors: Commissioning typically occurs with trusted devices
User must initiate commissioning with rogue device
Matter passcode has ~27 bits entropy, still requires effort

observations.title: Strong Mutual Authentication via SPAKE2+
observations.description: The FSM correctly implements bidirectional confirmation verification (cB by initiator, cA by responder) with explicit error paths for verification failures. This provides robust mutual authentication.

observations.title: Proper Session Key Isolation
observations.description: Session keys (I2RKey, R2IKey, AttestationChallenge) are derived locally and never transmitted. The FSM correctly separates key derivation from message transmission.

observations.title: Incomplete Coverage of Post-Session Properties
observations.description: Several properties (PROP_012, PROP_020, PROP_021, PROP_026, PROP_036, PROP_040) are marked UNVERIFIABLE because they concern behavior outside the PASE session establishment FSM scope.
observations.limitation: FSM abstraction ends at session establishment; post-session behavior requires separate modeling

observations.title: Timing Constraint Not Modeled
observations.description: The FSM has timing_requirement: null for all transitions. While the spec doesn't mandate strict timing, implementations may need timeout handling for stuck sessions.
observations.limitation: FSM does not model session timeout or abort on prolonged inactivity

proverif_verification_queries.fsm_correspondence: No transition sends passcode; only derived values pA, pB, cA, cB transmitted

proverif_verification_queries.expected_result: false (Ke secret from passive attacker without passcode)
proverif_verification_queries.fsm_correspondence: Ke computed via Crypto_P2 from shared secret; never transmitted

proverif_verification_queries.proverif_query: query i:principal, r:principal; event(pase_complete(i, r)) ==> event(mutual_auth(i, r)).
proverif_verification_queries.expected_result: true (both parties authenticated before session established)
proverif_verification_queries.fsm_correspondence: T12 (cB verified) + T15 (cA verified) both required before session states
